_comment: Bucket dictionary for /z-plan. The planner never eyeballs token counts (ESTIMATION.md: 'this is arithmetic, so compute it'). It picks ONE tier per ticket by the ESTIMATION.md model rules-of-thumb, copies that tier's entry verbatim into a buckets.json, and shells it to bin/z-estimate for the dollar figure. Same tier -> same buckets -> same z-estimate output, so estimates are reproducible across runs (issue #7 AC2). Each tier's buckets are directional full-lifecycle volumes (plan + build + QA + review + merge, per ESTIMATION.md step 3). tests/plan-schema.test.ts pins the resulting dollar total of every tier so a rate or bucket change fails the gate instead of silently drifting from SKILL.md. Calibrated 2026-07-20 (issue #81) from measured loop-run actuals: the original buckets undercounted real spend 3.3x board-wide (haiku-low/sonnet-medium/opus-high buckets modeled 0.7-1.2M cached tokens/ticket; measured medians were 10.3M-20.3M -- cached volume scales with turn-count squared since every turn re-sends the conversation, and stages are tool-call heavy). Derivation recipe (reproducible over the same transcript set, issue #7 AC2's determinism rule): for each Done ticket with a transcript dir under ~/.zstack/projects/<slug>/loop/transcripts/, run `z-cost --json '<dir>/*.jsonl'`, group results by the board's Model + Model Effort tier, take the median of each of fresh_input/cached_input/output across that tier's tickets, round each to the nearest 5000. haiku-low (n=2), sonnet-medium (n=15), and opus-high (n=8) have measured transcripts; opus-xhigh and fable-xhigh have none yet, so their buckets are the old ones scaled by the per-bucket correction factor measured on opus-high (new/old, computed before rounding: output ~x0.09, fresh_input ~x2.67, cached_input ~x8.56), then rounded to 5000 the same way. Output tokens were cut (not raised) across every tier -- cost does not scale with code written, it scales with turns times context size, and the old buckets assumed 5-16x too much output.
